overallInterpretation: The screenshot (tilemap-carved-visual.png) shows an interactive tilemap demo in its carving state. A 12x12 grid of solid green tiles occupies the lower-left portion of the canvas against a dark navy background. Two carved regions are clearly visible: a large roughly circular hole in the lower-left quadrant and a smaller carved area near the bottom-center. In both carved regions, tiles have been removed and the dark background is visible through the gaps. Red debug collision-edge outlines trace the jagged stair-step boundaries around the carved holes. A HUD overlay in the top-left displays mode (PAINT: Full), debug state (ON), grid dimensions (12x12), chunk count (1), and keyboard controls including C for carve and +/- for radius. The carving feature is functioning as expected: tiles are removed in approximate circle shapes, the background shows through, and collision edges are drawn around the carved boundaries.

artifacts.interpretation: Shows a green tilemap grid with two visible carved holes where tiles have been removed, exposing the dark background beneath. Red collision-edge outlines trace the perimeter of each carved area in a stair-step pattern following tile boundaries. A HUD overlay confirms debug mode is on and the grid is 12x12 with 1 chunk. The carving operation has clearly produced circular-ish removal patterns consistent with a radius-based carve tool.
